channel.title: Metals Industry News Feed
channel.description: Automated news feed for aluminum, steel, copper, and nickel industries

item.title: As Electricity Demand Increases, Steel Mills Hold a Big Bargaining Chip
item.description: Industry executives discuss concerns that electric utilities may prioritize data center needs over steel mill power demands, though fears of sudden prioritization are likely unfounded.

item.title: Ramp-Up of New Ternium Lines Moving Quicker Than Expected
item.description: Latin American steel producer Ternium is ramping up the new cold mill and galvanizing line at its Pesquería facility in Mexico faster than anticipated and expects to start the facility's new steel shop later this year.

item.title: Tenaris Warns That The Middle East Conflict Will Affect Second-Quarter Sales
item.description: Tenaris, an Italian steel pipe manufacturer, warned that its second-quarter sales will be impacted by lower shipments in the Middle East due to the ongoing conflict and the blockade of the Strait of Hormuz from the U.S. and Israel war against Iran. The company anticipates higher logistic costs affecting margins, but expects sales and margins to recover in the second half of the year if the waterway reopens. First-quarter sales rose 6% to $3.1 billion, driven by strong North American growth offsetting declines in Asia Pacific and the Middle East.

item.title: India's NMDC Raises Iron Ore Prices for Fourth Straight Month
item.description: Indian state-controlled miner NMDC increased iron ore prices in May despite pressure on domestic steel prices from slow demand. The price for 10-40mm Baila 65.5% lump ore was set at 5,500 rupees/t. Meanwhile, Indian hot-rolled coil prices fell from a near three-year high due to labor shortages, tighter financing, and anticipated corrections.

item.title: American Steel and Aluminum Opens New 50,000-Square-Foot Facility
item.description: American Steel and Aluminum announced the opening of a new 50,000-square-foot facility in Syracuse, N.Y., USA. This expansion represents growth in domestic steel production capacity.

item.title: Tenaris Announces CEO Succession
item.description: Tenaris's Board of Directors has appointed Gabriel Podskubka as Chief Executive Officer, while Paolo Rocca will continue to serve as Chairman of the Board. This marks a leadership transition for the company.

item.title: Canada's MASSIVE Aluminum Discovery Is ...
item.description: Canada announces a massive aluminum discovery near Saskatchewan with early estimates of 6.8 billion tons of alumina-bearing material, part of 50 billion tons of critical minerals including scandium and vanadium. The deposit could support mining for over 2,000 years at 50,000 tons per day, potentially enabling 1.88 million tons of annual aluminum production over 25 years.

item.title: RAME™: Breakthrough Salt-Free Dross Processing Technology for Primary Smelters
item.description: Altek introduces RAME™, a revolutionary salt-free dross processing technology designed for primary aluminum smelters. This innovation represents a significant advancement in sustainable aluminum production methods, addressing environmental concerns in the industry.

item.title: U.S. Air Force Awards Contract for 38 Advanced Aerospace Heat Treating Furnaces
item.description: Phillips Corporation Federal Division and DELTA H® Technologies have been awarded a U.S. Air Force contract to provide 38 advanced aerospace heat treating furnaces. This contract represents technological innovation in aluminum processing for aerospace applications.

item.title: Midal Cables Launches Third Edition of Sustainability Report
item.description: Midal Cables announces the release of its third sustainability report, demonstrating the company's continued commitment to structured sustainability practices in the aluminum industry. This milestone reflects ongoing efforts to integrate environmental responsibility into aluminum operations.

item.title: New Downstream Aluminum Fabrication Facility Planned in Inola, Oklahoma
item.description: A new downstream aluminum fabrication facility is being developed in Inola, Oklahoma, to convert molten primary aluminum into value-added products. This expansion represents innovation in aluminum processing and manufacturing capabilities.

item.title: Steel Rebar Futures Surge Above CNY 3,200 Per Ton
item.description: Steel rose to 3,240 CNY/T, reaching its strongest level since August 2025, supported by robust buying in mainland China as trading resumed after the May Day holiday. The surge is reinforced by a continued drawdown in steel inventories, which have fallen for seven consecutive weeks, pointing to tightening near-term supply conditions in the physical market.

item.title: Strong North American Buyer Interest Drives Steel Import Orders Higher
item.description: Steel traders report strong interest from North American buyers, with import orders ticking higher according to latest survey results. Sheet and plate prices remained on an upward trend as industry sources increasingly attribute market gains to improved demand alongside limited supply.

item.title: US crude steel production improved slightly last week, with the capacity utilisation rate climbing to 80.4%
item.description: US crude steel production saw a slight improvement last week, with the capacity utilization rate reaching 80.4%, the highest since August 2024. This marks a positive development in domestic steel output amid ongoing industry challenges.

item.title: US imposes sanctions on Congo armed group, mining firms over illicit minerals
item.description: The United States announced sanctions against an armed group and mining firms in Congo involved in illicit minerals trade, which impacts copper production in the region. This action targets disruptions in the supply chain from conflict areas. The measures aim to curb illegal activities affecting global copper output.

item.title: World's Biggest Nickel Mine in Indonesia Told to Slash Output
item.description: PT Weda Bay Nickel, the world's largest nickel mine, has been notified of a drastic production quota cut to 12 million tons for the year, down from 42 million tons in 2025. The Indonesian government is implementing these cuts to boost global prices of its largest export commodity, though the company intends to apply for a revision.

item.title: Tenaris Awarded Three Roberto Rocca Scholarships to Engineering Students
item.description: Tenaris has awarded three Roberto Rocca Scholarships to engineering students from King Fahd University of Petroleum & Minerals in Saudi Arabia. This initiative reinforces the company's commitment to developing local engineering talent in the Kingdom.

item.title: Tenaris Publishes 2025 Sustainability Statement
item.description: Tenaris has published an executive summary of its 2025 Sustainability Statement included in its annual report. The statement outlines how sustainability is embedded in the company's industrial strategy amid a complex geopolitical and market environment.

item.title: Tenaris Receives Ninth Consecutive worldsteel Sustainability Champions Recognition
item.description: Tenaris has received its ninth consecutive worldsteel Sustainability Champions recognition, demonstrating ongoing commitment to sustainable practices in the steel industry.

item.title: Tenaris Announces $11 Million USD Digitalization and Automation Investment
item.description: Tenaris has announced a significant $11 million USD investment in digitalization and automation initiatives to enhance operational efficiency.

item.title: Aluminum Falls to 3483.23 USD/T on May 7, 2026
item.description: Aluminum prices fell to 3483.23 USD/T on May 7, 2026, marking a 1.21% decline from the previous day. Over the past month, prices have risen 0.15%, and are up 44.61% over the past year. UK aluminum futures rebounded to $3,580 per tonne from a three-week low amid expectations of prolonged supply disruptions from the Middle East.

item.title: HRC Steel reaches highest level since January 2024 at 1,100 USD/T
item.description: Hot-Rolled Coil Steel increased to 1,100.00 USD/T, marking the highest price since January 2024. Over the past 4 weeks, HRC Steel gained 3.79%, demonstrating continued strength in the flat steel market.

item.title: US Structural Steel Pricing Update
item.description: Current structural steel pricing stands at $53.06 cwt as of May 7, 2026, reflecting recent market movements in the US steel sector.

item.title: Steel rebar futures surge above CNY 3,200 per ton, reaching strongest level since August 2025
item.description: Steel rebar futures surged above CNY 3,200 per ton, hitting their strongest level since August 2025, supported by robust buying in mainland China as trading resumed after the May Day holiday. The rally was reinforced by a continued drawdown in steel inventories, which have fallen for seven consecutive weeks, pointing to tightening near-term supply conditions.

item.title: North American East Coast export market firms with HMS 85/15 sale at $415.50 per metric ton
item.description: The export market on the North American East Coast has been firming after seeing a sale that climbed to $415.50 per metric ton CFR for HMS 85/15. Ternium expects steel demand in Mexico to continue improving through the second quarter as destocking fades.

item.title: Copper futures remain above $5.95 per pound on Friday after rebounding
item.description: Copper futures remained above $5.95 per pound on Friday after rebounding in the previous session, supported by signs of improving demand in China despite ongoing headwinds from the Middle East conflict. Copper futures in the US rose to over $6.1 per pound, the highest since reaching a record-close of $6.2 on January 29th as energy prices sank.

item.title: Copper Market Supply Constraints: Mitsui & Co and Fortescue Secure Major Offtake Deals
item.description: Mitsui & Co acquired 40% offtake rights to Argentina's Josemaria deposit, while Fortescue completed acquisition of Peru's Cañariaco project for approximately $101 million. These deals reflect major companies racing to secure copper supply amid market constraints and record-low treatment charges.

item.title: Supply Disruptions Impact Copper Production: Chile Refining Challenges and Indonesia's Grasberg Mine
item.description: Chile's copper production faces uncertainty due to Middle East conflict disrupting sulphur shipments to China, affecting roughly half of Chile's copper refining operations. Additionally, Indonesia's Grasberg mine continues operating below capacity following a fatal mudslide that triggered a force majeure event.

item.title: Let's dive into this new series of articles on sustainability, treatment processes and innovations in nickel production, starting with nickel ores
item.description: The Nickel Institute launches a new blog series exploring sustainability, advanced treatment processes, and innovations in nickel production, beginning with nickel ores. This initiative provides expert insights into improving environmental impact and technological advancements. It aims to inform industry stakeholders on sustainable practices.

item.title: Aluminum Futures Rebound Amid Prolonged Supply Disruptions from Middle East
item.description: Aluminum futures in the UK rebounded to $3,500 per tonne from a three-week low of $3,480 on April 29th, driven by expectations of ongoing supply disruptions from the Middle East. US President Trump indicated that the naval blockade on Iranian commercial vessels exiting the Strait of Hormuz would continue longer than anticipated. The price rose to 3,503.80 USD/T on May 8, up 0.59% from the previous day.

item.title: Aluminum rose to 3503.80 USD/T on May 8, 2026
item.description: Aluminum prices rebounded to $3,500 per tonne amid expectations of prolonged supply disruptions from the Middle East. US President Trump signaled that the naval blockade would remain longer for Iranian commercial vessels, with aluminum futures hovering near a four-year high as the Strait of Hormuz blockage threatens continued supply disruptions.

item.title: Steel - Price - Chart - Historical Data - News
item.description: Steel prices fell to 3,244 CNY/T on May 8, 2026, down 0.22% from the previous day, though up 5.63% over the past month and 6.40% year-over-year. Steel rebar futures surged to around CNY 3,250 per ton, the strongest since August last year, driven by robust buying in China post-May Day holiday and seven consecutive weeks of inventory drawdowns indicating tightening supply. Market sentiment is bolstered by strong seasonal demand and restocking.

item.title: Copper futures remained above $5.95 per pound on Friday after rebounding in the previous session
item.description: Copper futures stayed above $5.95 per pound on Friday, supported by signs of improving demand in China. This follows a rebound in the previous session despite ongoing headwinds from the Middle East conflict. The price rose to 6.25 USD/Lbs on May 8, up 2.04% from the previous day.

item.title: Nickel Futures Surge Near 2-Year High
item.description: Nickel futures traded above $19,500 per tonne, nearing a two-year high, driven by supply disruptions and tightening raw material availability. Mining quota cuts in Indonesia have constrained ore supply, while a sulfur shortage linked to disruptions in the Strait of Hormuz has raised processing costs for nickel intermediates.

item.title: Nickel Prices Pull Back from Recent Rally as Profit-Taking Emerges
item.description: Nickel fell to $18,878 USD/T on May 8, 2026, down 1.24% from the previous day, as traders locked in gains after the recent sharp rally. The pullback reflects profit-taking rather than deterioration in underlying fundamentals, with broader industrial metals showing mixed price action.

item.title: Nickel Futures Price Today
item.description: Nickel futures are currently trading at $18,906.50, down from a previous close of $19,083.25. Despite recent gains, UBS has warned of a persistent surplus in the global nickel market through 2026.

item.title: Nickel breakthrough: EV batteries become 84% greener with new zero-carbon process
item.description: A new nickel extraction process enables single‑step recovery from low‑grade ores with up to 84% lower emissions, offering a cleaner pathway for nickel used in electric vehicle batteries. The technology could significantly reduce the carbon footprint of EV supply chains while improving efficiency in nickel production.

item.title: Aluminum rose to 3,503.05 USD/T on May 8, 2026
item.description: Aluminum prices rose to 3,503.05 USD/T, up 0.57% from the previous day. Over the past month, aluminum's price has risen 1.64%, and is up 44.69% year-over-year. The rebound is attributed to expectations of prolonged supply disruptions from the Middle East.

item.title: Aluminium Futures at 3,503.05 - Technical Analysis
item.description: Aluminium futures are trading at 3,503.05 with a previous close of 3,483.23. Technical analysis suggests aluminum is approaching key support zones with potential for bullish rebound if buyers regain control.

item.title: Steel fell to 3244 CNY/T on May 8, 2026
item.description: Steel prices declined 0.22% to 3,244 CNY/T, though prices have risen 5.63% over the past month and 6.40% year-over-year. Steel rebar futures surged to around CNY 3,250 per ton, reaching their strongest level since August 2025, supported by robust buying in mainland China and a continued seven-week drawdown in steel inventories.

item.title: Copper rises to 6.23 USD/Lbs, up 1.62% on May 8
item.description: Copper rose to 6.23 USD/Lbs on May 8, 2026, up 1.62% from the previous day. Over the past month, copper's price has risen 8.33%, and is up 35.18% compared to the same period last year.

item.title: Aluminum Price Reaches $3,503.05 USD/T Amid Supply Disruptions
item.description: Aluminum prices rose to $3,503.05 USD/T on May 8, 2026, up 0.57% from the previous day, with a 44.69% increase year-over-year. The price surge is driven by prolonged supply disruptions from the Middle East due to the naval blockade of the Strait of Hormuz, while strong manufacturing activity from China supports demand.

item.title: Steel Price Falls Slightly but Rebar Futures Surge Post-Holiday
item.description: Steel prices fell to 3,244 CNY/T on May 8, down 0.22% from the previous day, though up 5.63% over the past month and 6.40% year-over-year. Steel rebar futures surged to around CNY 3,250 per ton, the strongest since August last year, driven by robust buying in China after the May Day holiday and seven consecutive weeks of inventory drawdowns indicating tightening supply.

item.title: HRC Steel Price Dips Marginally Amid Recent Gains
item.description: HRC Steel fell to 1,130 USD/T on May 8, down 0.09% from the previous day, after reaching 1,100 USD/T, the highest since January 2024. Over the past four weeks, prices gained 3.79%, reflecting ongoing strength in the market.

item.title: Copper futures remained above $5.95 per pound on Friday after rebounding
item.description: Copper futures stayed above $5.95 per pound on Friday, rebounding in the previous session due to signs of improving demand in China. This occurred despite headwinds from the Middle East conflict. Prices rose to 6.25 USD/Lbs on May 8, up 1.98% from the previous day.

item.title: Powering the 21st Century with American Copper
item.description: A video discussing American copper production and its role in powering modern infrastructure and technology in the 21st century.

item.title: Freeport now aims for 65% capacity in the second half of 2026, 80% by mid-2027, and near full capacity by the end of 2027
item.description: Freeport has updated its production ramp-up targets for nickel operations, aiming for 65% capacity utilization in the second half of 2026, increasing to 80% by mid-2027 and nearing full capacity by the end of 2027. This reflects ongoing efforts to expand nickel output amid global demand pressures. The announcement highlights challenges and timelines in achieving higher production levels.

item.title: EU Mills Sustain Steel Prices Through Trade Barriers
item.description: EU mills are maintaining steel prices via trade barriers rather than stronger demand, as weak manufacturing sentiment persists amid geopolitical tensions, US tariffs, and CBAM uncertainty. Italian distributors reported lower sales and prices in April year-over-year for long and flat products. Concerns rise ahead of revised EU safeguard measures effective July 1, reducing import quotas by 47%.

item.title: Freeport now aims for 65% capacity in the second half of 2026
item.description: Freeport aims to reach 65% production capacity in the second half of 2026, 80% by mid-2027, and near full capacity by the end of 2027. This update pertains to nickel production capacity expansion. The announcement reflects ongoing efforts to ramp up output amid market dynamics.

item.title: Danieli to Upgrade QSP Complex for EZZ Flat Steel
item.description: Danieli has been selected by EZZ Flat Steel to modernize its quality strip production (QSP®) casting and rolling complex at Ain Sokhna works in Egypt. This upgrade represents a significant technological advancement in flat steel production.

item.title: Chilean miner Codelco’s El Teniente smelter to restart after collapse
item.description: Chilean miner Codelco’s smelter at its El Teniente mine is set to restart on Thursday after a collapse. This development could impact copper supply from one of the world's largest producers. The announcement comes amid rising copper prices.

item.title: China state firm discusses major new copper mine in Congo
item.description: China Railway Group is proposing a major new copper mine project in Congo expected to produce between 200,000 and 500,000 tons of copper annually. This represents significant capacity expansion in African copper production.

item.title: Copper supply under pressure: Should prices be higher?
item.description: Multiple top-tier copper mines are facing ongoing supply disruptions, creating tight supply-demand dynamics. Frazer Tabeart from Alma Metals discusses how supply risks are amplifying price pressures in the copper market.

item.title: Supply disruptions at copper mines driving higher prices
item.description: Mining analyst Sam Crittenden from RBC Capital Markets discusses the copper bull run driven by supply disruptions at major mines. The analysis covers current market dynamics and preferred copper stocks.

item.title: Copper Prices Reach Record Amid Supply Squeeze and Tariff Fears
item.description: Copper prices have reached record levels as multiple mines experience supply disruptions and tariff concerns emerge. Industrial activity and shipping dynamics are influencing market conditions.

item.title: Tenaris Board Appoints Gabriel Podskubka as Chief Executive Officer
item.description: Tenaris announced that its Board of Directors appointed Gabriel Podskubka as Chief Executive Officer. Paolo Rocca will continue to serve as Chairman of the Board.

item.title: Tenaris Awards Roberto Rocca Scholarships to Saudi Engineering Students
item.description: Tenaris has awarded three Roberto Rocca Scholarships to engineering students from King Fahd University of Petroleum & Minerals in Saudi Arabia, reinforcing its commitment to developing local engineering talent in the Kingdom.

item.title: Copper futures remain above $5.95 per pound supported by China demand
item.description: Copper futures remained above $5.95 per pound on Friday after rebounding in the previous session. The market was supported by signs of improving demand in China despite ongoing headwinds from the Middle East conflict.

item.title: Mining with Integrity to Advance Sustainable Nickel Downstreaming
item.description: Indonesia is pushing for more sustainable nickel downstreaming by emphasizing mining integrity, emissions reductions, and lower carbon intensity in nickel products, with targets to cut emissions by 33% by 2030 and reduce carbon intensity by up to 50%. The article underscores how Indonesia’s 2020 nickel ore export ban has reshaped its strategy toward value-added, cleaner nickel production.

item.title: New Technology Set to Revolutionize Nickel Pollution Cleanup
item.description: A new technology using olivine, a naturally occurring mineral, is being developed to clean up nickel-related pollution and permanently store carbon dioxide, offering a dual solution for environmental remediation and climate mitigation. The approach could transform how the nickel industry manages its environmental footprint, especially in mining and processing regions.

item.title: US Raw Steel Production and Capability Utilization Report
item.description: In the week ending May 9, 2026, domestic raw steel production reached 1,880,000 net tons with a capability utilization rate of 81.4 percent. This represents continued growth in US steel production capacity.

item.title: Industry Data - Week Ending May 9, 2026
item.description: Domestic raw steel production reached 1,880,000 net tons with a capability utilization rate of 81.4% for the week ending May 9, 2026. Production was broken down by district: North East (140k tons), Great Lakes (522k tons), Midwest (309k tons), Southern (842k tons), and Western (67k tons).

item.title: Indonesia on track to become largest global producer of refined nickel
item.description: Indonesia is positioned to become the world's largest refined nickel producer, a critical mineral for lithium-ion batteries and steel production. However, analysis reveals that over 75% of the country's refining capacity is controlled by Chinese stakeholders, many with ties to the CCP.

item.title: How the Nickel Industry Is Rebuilding Supply Outside Indonesia
item.description: Supply concentration in nickel, particularly in Indonesia, is driving price increases and strengthening investment cases for large-scale sulphide projects outside the country. The industry is actively diversifying production capacity away from Indonesia.

item.title: Tenaris to Acquire Artrom Steel Tubes S.A.
item.description: Tenaris has entered into a definitive agreement to acquire 100% of the share capital of Artrom Steel Tubes S.A. from GLGH Steel, LLC, a U.S.-based company. The acquisition is expected to expand Tenaris's industrial pipe product range and manufacturing footprint, strengthening its ability to serve customers.

item.title: Gabriel Podskubka Appointed as Chief Executive Officer
item.description: Tenaris's Board of Directors has appointed Gabriel Podskubka as Chief Executive Officer. Paolo Rocca will continue to serve as Chairman of the Board.

item.title: 2025 Sustainability Statement Published
item.description: Tenaris has published an executive summary of its 2025 Sustainability Statement included in its annual report. The statement outlines how sustainability is embedded in the company's industrial strategy amid a complex geopolitical and market environment.

item.title: Aluminum - Price - Chart - Historical Data - News
item.description: Aluminum rose to 3,578.45 USD/T on May 11, 2026, up 2.15% from the previous day, despite a 1.33% monthly decline. UK aluminum futures rebounded to $3,530 per tonne from a three-week low amid expectations of prolonged Middle East supply disruptions.

item.title: HRC Steel Falls to 1,121.10 USD/T on May 11, 2026
item.description: HRC Steel decreased to 1,121.10 USD/T on May 11, 2026, down 0.79% from the previous day, but recently hit 1100.00 USD/T, the highest since January 2024. Over the past four weeks, prices gained 3.79%, reflecting positive trends in hot-rolled coil steel.

item.title: US Mills' Capability Utilization Hits 81.4% Last Week
item.description: US steel mills' capability utilization reached 81.4% last week, up from 80.4% the prior week and 76.6% a year ago, with raw steel output continuing to rise. The May scrap market settled with mixed prime tags after a prolonged process. SSAB increased transaction prices for plate orders shipping from June 28, and Nucor hiked spot HR prices by $10/ton.

item.title: Alcoa announces USD 65 million investment to enhance aluminum production
item.description: Alcoa Corporation, one of the world's most sustainable aluminum producers, announced a USD 65 million investment to enhance its aluminum production capabilities. This investment reflects the company's commitment to advancing its operations.

item.title: Report: China, India Control the Fate of Steel Decarbonization
item.description: The global steel industry's decarbonization effort is slowly progressing, but whether the pace accelerates or stalls will largely depend on China and India. This report highlights the critical role these two nations play in determining the future of sustainable steel production worldwide.

item.title: Steel Price Update: Rose to 3,241 CNY/T on May 12, 2026
item.description: Steel prices rose to 3,241 CNY/T on May 12, 2026, up 0.12% from the previous day, with a 5.23% increase over the past month. Steel rebar futures climbed toward CNY 3,250 per ton amid expectations for stronger demand, stable production in China, and improved mill profit margins. Stronger economic data and surging exports further supported prices.

item.title: Steel Falls to 3,226 CNY/T on May 12, 2026
item.description: Steel prices fell to 3,226 CNY/T on May 12, 2026, down 0.34% from the previous day, though up 4.74% over the past month. Steel rebar futures surged to around CNY 3,250 per ton after the May Day holiday, bolstered by robust buying and a seven-week drawdown in inventories indicating tightening supply.

item.title: Nickel Prices Today 2026 | Live Chart, Battery Grade & Stainless Steel
item.description: Global nickel prices advanced 1.38% to $19,204.64 per ton, with Chinese prices at ¥130,528 per ton, driven by supply constraints. Indonesia slashed mining quotas, tightening ore availability, while a global sulfur shortage from Middle East tensions threatens MHP processing. A weaker US dollar and firm SHFE quotes provide further bullish support.

item.title: Aluminum Fell to 3,575.20 USD/T on May 12, 2026
item.description: Aluminum prices declined to 3,575.20 USD/T, down 0.09% from the previous day. Over the past month, aluminum's price has fallen 1.42%, though it remains significantly higher at 43.53% above year-ago levels.

item.title: HRC Steel Falls to 1,129.04 USD/T
item.description: Hot-Rolled Coil Steel declined 0.52% to 1,129.04 USD/T on May 12, 2026. Despite the daily decline, HRC Steel has gained 5% over the past 4 weeks and reached its highest level since January 2024 at 1,135.00 USD/T.

item.title: Steel Market Update: Prices Increase $5-25 per Short Ton
item.description: Steel prices increased between $5-25 per short ton from the previous week and are $30-65 per short ton higher than one month ago. The market continues to show upward momentum in pricing.

item.title: West Asia Has 10% Global Aluminium Capacity, Most Remains...
item.description: Nirmal Bang Securities analyst Kunal Shah discusses aluminum prices at 4-year highs, with Goldman Sachs and Citi projecting prices could reach 3,600 in the next 3 months. Supply concerns from the Middle East and China's planned smelting capacity cuts are driving bullish price trends.

item.title: Century Aluminum sees higher demand, restarts capacity, and plans 750,000 tonne smelter
item.description: Century Aluminum, with total production capacity of 770,000 tonnes per year, produced approximately 638,000 tonnes of primary aluminum in 2025. The company is responding to higher demand by restarting capacity and planning expansion.

item.title: Scientists create a new revolutionary aluminum that could slash industrial costs and accelerate the energy transition
item.description: Scientists have developed a new aluminum with innovative properties that could redefine production processes and significantly reduce dependence on critical metals. This breakthrough technology has the potential to substantially lower industrial costs while supporting the energy transition.

item.title: Steel Prices Rise 0.06% to 3,211 CNY/T
item.description: Steel prices in China rose to 3,211 CNY/T with a 0.06% increase from the previous day. Over the past month, steel prices have climbed 4.19%, and are up 2.36% compared to the same period last year.

item.title: US Steel Mill Outages Unlikely to Shift Market
item.description: Steel mill outages through the remainder of 2026 are expected to remove nearly 600,000 short tons of flat-rolled production capacity. Industry analysts believe these outages will have limited impact on overall market dynamics.

item.title: Copper Futures Reach Fresh All-Time Highs
item.description: Copper futures climbed to around $6.6 per pound, reaching fresh all-time highs on the back of stronger Chinese demand and growing supply concerns. The rally reflects both increased global demand and tightening supply conditions.

item.title: Breaking down sudden surge of nickel prices
item.description: The London Metal Exchange suspended nickel trading after prices more than doubled to surpass $100,000 per metric ton due to extreme price moves in Asian trading hours. The LME stated that trading would be suspended for at least the remainder of the day.

item.title: Nickel rose to 19,097.75 USD/T
item.description: Nickel reached $19,097.75 per metric ton on May 13, 2026, up 0.91% from the previous day. Over the past month, nickel's price has risen 4.88% and is up 20.87% year-to-date.

item.title: New EV battery recycling process recovers 99% pure nickel and cobalt
item.description: Researchers have developed an eco-friendly recycling technology that extracts 99 percent pure nickel and cobalt from waste batteries. This innovation addresses sustainability concerns in the EV battery supply chain by enabling high-purity material recovery from end-of-life batteries.

item.title: Cogne Acciai Speciali: 94% of energy from renewable sources, 93% permanent employment
item.description: Cogne Acciai Speciali highlighted its sustainability initiatives, reporting that 94% of energy used comes from renewable sources and 93% of employees have permanent contracts, positioning itself as a conscious industrial player.

item.title: Tenaris enters into definitive agreement to acquire GLGH Steel, LLC
item.description: Tenaris has announced a definitive agreement to acquire 100% of the share capital of GLGH Steel, LLC, a U.S.-based company. This acquisition represents a strategic expansion of Tenaris's operations in the steel sector.

item.title: Copper Cables: Antitrust Launches Investigation into Alleged Cartel
item.description: Italy's Antitrust authority has opened an investigation against major low-voltage copper cable manufacturers, including Prysmian, for allegedly participating in anti-competitive agreements involving price fixing and coordinated sales practices since 2005. The investigation involves nine companies and the Italian Association of Electrical Cable and Conductor Industries (AICE). Prysmian's stock price declined 1.26% following the news.

item.title: US Producer Prices Surge 6% in April on Rising Energy Costs from Mideast Conflict
item.description: Prices paid to US producers jumped 6% annually in April, the largest gain since December 2022, driven by elevated energy costs amid the Mideast Gulf war. The producer price index rose from a revised 4.3% in March, according to the Bureau of Labor Statistics. This surge impacts steel and related sectors.

item.title: Nickel Rises 1.27% to $19,165 Per Tonne Amid Supply Disruptions
item.description: Nickel futures traded above $19,500 per tonne, nearing a two-year high, driven by mining quota cuts in Indonesia and sulfur shortages linked to Strait of Hormuz disruptions. These supply constraints have tightened raw material availability and raised processing costs for nickel intermediates, reinforcing bullish momentum in the market.

item.title: 4 MIN AGO: Canada Uncovers Massive Aluminum Find
item.description: Canada has discovered a massive alumina/aluminum-related deposit in Saskatchewan, potentially transforming North America's supply chain, manufacturing, and critical minerals strategy. The find could impact global aluminum supply amid trade tensions and rising demand. Countries are closely watching as it addresses import dependence.

item.title: Steel Falls to 3209 CNY/T Amid Expectations of Seasonal Demand Slowdown
item.description: Steel prices fell to 3209 CNY/T on May 14, 2026, down 0.12% from the previous day, as rebar futures pulled back from nine-month highs due to anticipated seasonal slowdown in demand and softer downstream consumption. Mysteel's monthly report highlights pressure on construction steel prices from weakening supply-demand fundamentals. Over the past month, prices have risen 3.72%.

item.title: SMU Sheet and Plate Price Indices Rise to New Multi-Year Highs
item.description: All five of SMU's sheet and plate price indices increased this week, reaching new multi-year highs with gains of $5-25 per short ton from last week. Prices are now $30-65 per short ton higher than one month ago, signaling strong upward momentum in the US market.

item.title: Latest Copper News
item.description: Recent developments in copper mining emphasize sustainable practices and the exploration of new deposits. Chile, as the world's largest copper producer, is central to these efforts. This reflects the ongoing push for sustainability in the industry.

item.title: Nickel Falls 0.78% as Profit-Taking Emerges After Recent Rally
item.description: Nickel futures fell to $18,842.50 per tonne, down 0.78% from the previous day, as traders locked in gains following a sharp rally that brought prices near a two-year high of $19,600. The pullback reflects profit-taking rather than deterioration in fundamentals, with broader industrial metals showing mixed price action after a strong run across the base metals complex.

item.title: Nickel Futures Price Today - Market Update
item.description: Nickel futures are currently trading at $18,871.75, down from the previous close of $19,198.50. The market outlook remains bearish according to UBS, which warns of persistent surplus conditions expected through 2026 despite recent production cuts.

item.title: New recycling tech recovers nearly pure nickel and cobalt from waste batteries
item.description: Researchers have developed an eco-friendly recycling technology that extracts 99 percent pure nickel and cobalt from waste batteries. This innovation addresses sustainability concerns in battery recycling and material recovery for the EV industry.

item.title: Innovations in Sustainable Nickel Production: The Path to Low-Cost Solutions
item.description: The global nickel market is undergoing rapid changes with innovations in mining and refining aimed at reducing production costs and enhancing environmental sustainability. These developments represent a shift toward more responsible nickel production practices.
